steps.title: Test if the sampling is random
steps.content: Random sampling requires every person in the population to have an equal chance of selection. Tabitha surveyed only at a playground. Families without children and non-playground visitors are systematically excluded. This violates the requirement for random sampling.

steps.title: Recognize the consequence: selection bias
steps.content: When sampling is non-random and location-based, the sample is not representative of the population. Playground families are likely to have more children than the full community. This creates selection bias. The sample estimate will be biased—in this case, systematically too high.

steps.title: Find the statement that must be true
steps.content: We determined that the sampling method is non-random (flawed) and produces a biased estimate. Choice C states exactly this: The sampling method is flawed and may produce a biased estimate of the mean. That matches choice C — C is correct.
